The Legend

Chuck Norris
1940 — 2026

Martial artist. Actor. Icon. From his six-time World Karate Championship to Walker, Texas Ranger, Chuck Norris became an enduring symbol of American toughness and heart. His legacy lives on through his family, his foundation, and the millions he inspired.

Visit the Chuck Norris Archive

The Legacy Collection

The Official Chuck Norris™ Kimono

Before the movies, before Walker, Texas Ranger, before the legend, there was a young martial artist who changed the sport forever. Chuck Norris earned black belts in Tang Soo Do, Taekwondo, Brazilian Jiu Jitsu, and Judo. He became the first man in the Western Hemisphere to be awarded an 8th degree Black Belt Grand Master in Tae Kwon Do.

He went on to win the Professional Middleweight Karate Championship, holding the title for six consecutive years. From that legacy, he founded the Chuck Norris System, his own martial arts discipline rooted in the principles of honor, discipline, and relentless self improvement.

Wearing this kimono means wearing the same gi that became a symbol of American martial arts excellence. Each piece is crafted with the same attention to detail as the originals Chuck wore in competition. The black collar, the American flag patch on the sleeve, the official Chuck Norris System badge. This is not a costume. This is a piece of martial arts history.
